QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_21

question.text: The interest earned on employee contribution to EPF in excess of which amount per year is taxable (from April 2021)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** From April 1, 2021, interest credited on employee's contribution in excess of Rs.2,50,000 per year (Rs.5,00,000 if no employer contribution) is taxable. **The Trap:** Rs.1,50,000 is the 80C limit. Rs.5,00,000 applies only when there is no employer contribution. For most employees with employer match, the threshold is Rs.2,50,000. **Cross-Exam Context:** EPF taxation changes post-2021 are tested in Series XVII and X-B. This affects high-salary employees contributing heavily to EPF/VPF. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For high-income clients contributing over Rs.2.5 lakh annually to EPF+VPF, the excess interest is now taxable. They may want to redirect excess contributions to NPS or other tax-efficient products.
